title: Pilosella capillata
taxonomy: Pilosella capillata is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is a member of the genus Pilosella, which is a group of plants previously classified as the genus Hieracium. The genus Pilosella is part of the tribe Cichorieae, which includes several genera of dandelion-like plants. Pilosella capillata is native to Europe, western Asia, and northwestern Africa. Its classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Asterales, Family: Asteraceae, Genus: Pilosella, Species: P. capillata.
uses: Pilosella capillata has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. The plant's leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. They can be used to treat various skin conditions, including wounds, eczema, and acne. Additionally, Pilosella capillata is believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, which may help to soothe irritated skin and prevent infections. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's full range of medicinal applications and potential uses.

intro: Pilosella capillata, also known as the woolly hawk's beard, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella capillata include sesquiterpene lactones, such as piloselin and dehydrohelanin, which are responsible for its bioactivity. The most common herbal preparations made with Pilosella capillata include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support digestive health and immune function.
